title: Rostellularia glandulosa
taxonomy: Rostellularia glandulosa is a species of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae. This plant is native to Australia and is known for its fragrant flowers and hairy leaves. Rostellularia glandulosa is classified as a perennial herb with small, white or purple flowers that bloom in clusters. The plant grows in a variety of habitats, including forests and grasslands, and is often found in rocky areas. Rostellularia glandulosa is also known by its common name of 'hairy mint bush'.
uses: Rostellularia glandulosa, also known as green kidney, has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions. Its leaves and stems are said to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of fever and rheumatism. In some cultures, the plant is used to treat digestive issues, such as diarrhea and constipation. The plant's antiseptic properties are also believed to aid in wound healing and skin infections. However, more research is needed to confirm the effectiveness of Rostellularia glandulosa in treating these conditions.
